title: Adopt generic ranking + realtime drag-and-drop on the work-items table
scope:
Wire the generic ranking primitives (useResourceRank, useOptimisticReorder, <DragHandleColumn>) onto the work-items table at app/(user)/work-items/page.tsx; subscribe to rankTopic("work_item", …) via useRefetchOnPush for cross-tab/user fan-out; verify the canonical ORDER BY coalesce(sprint_position, backlog_position) NULLS LAST, key_num ASC across all callers; cover with a Playwright spec; confirm parent-drag cascades children server-side.

The work-items table is the last first-class table not yet using the shared rank pattern landed in commit 43d7468.
value: Users (and gadmin/dev) gain consistent drag-to-reorder behaviour on work items in both backlog and per-sprint views, with reorders surviving reload and propagating to other tabs and users in real time. The change closes the generic-ranking adoption rollout and removes the last bespoke ordering surface in the app.
implementation_plan: Frontend: render <DragHandleColumn> as the new first cell in the work-items table; spread rank.rowProps(row.id) on each <tr>; pass onMoved/onError to the optimistic reorder hook.

Frontend: subscribe to rankTopic("work_item", subscriptionID, scope, scopeID) using useRefetchOnPush, mirroring the LayersTable pattern; scope is ("sprint", sprintID) when a sprint is selected, else ("backlog", subscriptionID).
Backend: add an integration test in backend/internal/workitems/service_test.go pinning the canonical ORDER BY for sprint, backlog, and my-vista list paths.
Frontend UX: confirm parent-drag PATCH body sends parent.id only; add a backend follow-up if cascade is missing.
DEV: add dev/tests/playwright/work-items-drag.spec.ts — leaf drag persistence + parent-drag subtree-bundle ghost; reuse helpers from rank-drag.spec.ts.
areas_impacted: UI: app/(user)/work-items/page.tsx — table render path, row props, drag handle column
API: backend/internal/workitems/service_test.go — new integration test for ORDER BY
SQL: no schema changes (work_items already has backlog_position + sprint_position)
feature_list: Drag-handle column on the work-items table
Optimistic reorder with rollback on backend error
Cross-tab/user realtime refresh via rankTopic subscription
Parent drag cascades children server-side; subtree-bundle ghost on the wire
features_extended: Generic ranking adopters: portfolio-model LayersTable → + work-items table
Playwright coverage extended from rank-drag.spec.ts to a work-items-drag.spec.ts mirror

work_item_backlog.title: Frontend: drag-handle column + reorder hooks on work-items table
Frontend: rankTopic subscription on work-items table
Backend: ORDER BY integration test for work_item callers
Frontend: parent-drag payload sends parent.id only
DEV: Playwright spec for work-items DnD persistence + subtree ghost
Frontend: rankTopic scope rewires on sprint selector change

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A new drag-handle column renders as the first cell of every work-items row, before the existing toggle column
Dragging a row issues a PATCH to the rank endpoint with the moved id and target position; table reflects the new order optimistically
Refreshing the page preserves post-drag order; on a forced backend error the optimistic move rolls back
app/(user)/work-items/page.tsx imports useRefetchOnPush and subscribes to rankTopic on mount
With tab A and tab B open on the same view, dragging a row in tab A causes tab B to refetch and display the new order within 1 second
Subscription scope tuple is ("sprint", sprintID) when a sprint is selected, else ("backlog", subscriptionID), mirroring LayersTable
service_test.go seeds rows with mixed sprint/backlog positions and asserts coalesce(sprint_position, backlog_position) NULLS LAST then key_num ASC across sprint/backlog/my-vista paths
Temporarily swapping the ORDER BY in service.go causes the test to fail
Test runs via go test ./backend/... and passes on current main
Dragging a parent row with expanded children issues a PATCH whose body contains a single id (parent's), no children ids
After the drag, refreshing the page shows the parent and all its children in their new position with parent-child links intact
Backend service confirmed to cascade on parent move; if cascade is missing, a backend story is filed and linked in the card comment before this story closes
dev/tests/playwright/work-items-drag.spec.ts exists, imports drag helpers from rank-drag.spec.ts, and runs against /work-items
A test drags a leaf row to a new position, reloads the page, and asserts the row index matches the post-drag position
A test expands a parent with children, drags the parent, and asserts the visual ghost contains the bundled subtree during the drag
useRefetchOnPush receives a topic computed from the current sprintID so React re-runs when sprintID changes
Switching the sprint selector emits an unsubscribe on the old channel and a subscribe on the new channel
Reordering rows in sprint A while viewing sprint B does not cause sprint B's table to refetch

risks.risk: Subtree cascade payload mismatch — frontend sends children ids while backend expects parent-only, causing concurrent reorders to split the subtree
risks.mitigation: Story 00234 explicitly asserts the PATCH body shape; Playwright spec 00235 asserts post-reload subtree integrity

risks.risk: rankTopic subscription churn when switching sprint scope causes flicker or duplicate refetches
risks.mitigation: useRefetchOnPush already debounces in LayersTable; story 00236 verifies unsubscribe-old + subscribe-new behaviour

risks.risk: ORDER BY drift in a future refactor silently scrambles list order before users notice
risks.mitigation: Story 00233 adds an integration test that fails if any caller regresses from the canonical clause
